name: PUREX Process
description: Plutonium Uranium Redox EXtraction process using tributyl phosphate (TBP) solvent extraction for separating uranium and plutonium from spent nuclear fuel
function_alignment: Direct match for uranium and plutonium extraction from spent fuel with established industrial scale capability
notes: Most mature and widely deployed reprocessing technology, currently operating at La Hague at similar capacity

verification.evidence:
<!doctype html><html lang="en"><head><meta charset="utf-8"><meta http-equiv="X-UA-Compatible" content="IE=edge"><meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1"><title>OpenAlex</title><script>// Suppress console errors for ResizeObserver
      const originalError = console.error;

gap_analysis: Perfect alignment - proven industrial technology for uranium/plutonium extraction from UOX, MOX, and ERU fuels at required capacity scale.

name: Hydrometallurgical Reprocessing
description: Aqueous-based chemical processing for reprocessing mixed uranium-plutonium nuclear fuel with short cooling times
function_alignment: Suitable for multiple fuel types including mixed U-Pu fuels, demonstrated for fast reactor fuels
notes: Proven technology but less mature than PUREX for large-scale operations, suitable for diverse fuel matrices
gap_analysis: Good alignment for multiple fuel types including mixed U-Pu fuels. Demonstrated for fast reactor fuels but limited large-scale commercial deployment.

name: Spent Fuel Dissolution and Reprocessing Processes
description: Comprehensive fuel dissolution and chemical separation processes for spent nuclear fuel treatment
function_alignment: Essential front-end process for any reprocessing system handling multiple fuel types
notes: Fundamental technology required for all reprocessing approaches, well-established for various fuel matrices

verification.evidence:
<!doctype html><html lang="en"><head><meta charset="utf-8"><meta http-equiv="X-UA-Compatible" content="IE=edge"><meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1"><title>OpenAlex</title><script>// Suppress console errors for ResizeObserver
      const originalError = console.error;

gap_analysis: Essential component of any reprocessing system. Covers critical front-end processes for multiple fuel types with established industrial implementation.

name: COEX Process
description: Co-extraction process for simultaneous recovery of uranium and plutonium without pure plutonium separation
domain: Nuclear fuel reprocessing - advanced solvent extraction
function_alignment: Advanced reprocessing technology with enhanced proliferation resistance while maintaining fissile material recovery
notes: Advanced evolution of PUREX with improved safeguards, suitable for industrial scale but less commercially proven
gap_analysis: Strong alignment with enhanced proliferation resistance. Achieves fissile material recovery function while addressing regulatory concerns.

name: GANEX Process
description: Grouped ActiNide EXtraction process for simultaneous extraction of uranium, plutonium, and minor actinides
function_alignment: Advanced process capable of recovering fissile materials while addressing minor actinide management
notes: Next-generation technology with broader scope than required, but lower maturity for near-term implementation

name: Remote Handling Systems for Nuclear Reprocessing
description: Automated and remotely operated equipment systems for handling radioactive materials in reprocessing facilities
function_alignment: Essential for ALARA compliance and operational availability in high-radiation reprocessing environment
notes: Mature supporting technology absolutely required for reprocessing operations, proven in existing facilities
gap_analysis: Essential enabling technology for ALARA compliance and operational availability. Critical for safe operation but not the core separation function.

name: Tributyl Phosphate (TBP) Solvent Extraction
description: Chemical solvent extraction technology using TBP in hydrocarbon diluent for actinide separation
function_alignment: Core separation technology for uranium and plutonium recovery with proven industrial scalability
notes: Well-established chemistry with decades of industrial application, forms basis of most commercial reprocessing

gap_analysis: Core separation chemistry for uranium and plutonium recovery. Fundamental basis for PUREX and related industrial processes.

name: Nuclear Fuel Reprocessing Plant Process Control Systems
description: Automated process control and monitoring systems for nuclear fuel reprocessing operations
function_alignment: Essential for maintaining product purity standards and operational availability requirements
notes: Mature technology critical for consistent operation and quality control in high-capacity facilities
gap_analysis: Essential for product purity standards and operational availability. Supporting technology rather than core separation function.

name: Multi-stage Centrifugal Contactors
description: High-efficiency liquid-liquid extraction equipment for solvent extraction processes in nuclear reprocessing
function_alignment: High-throughput separation equipment suitable for 1800 tHM/year capacity requirements
notes: Proven equipment technology for industrial-scale solvent extraction with compact design and high efficiency
gap_analysis: High-throughput separation equipment well-suited for 1800 tHM/year capacity requirements with proven industrial deployment.

name: Hot Cell Technology
description: Heavily shielded containment systems for handling highly radioactive materials with remote manipulation capabilities
function_alignment: Essential containment technology for ALARA compliance and radiological safety during reprocessing operations
notes: Fundamental and mature infrastructure technology for safe handling of highly radioactive materials
gap_analysis: Essential containment technology for ALARA compliance and radiological safety. Required infrastructure but not core separation function.

name: PUREX (Plutonium Uranium Redox Extraction) Process
description: Established aqueous reprocessing technology using tributyl phosphate (TBP) in kerosene to separate uranium and plutonium from fission products through solvent extraction. Widely implemented at industrial scale including La Hague facility.
function_alignment: Direct - primary industrial method for uranium/plutonium extraction from spent fuel with proven multi-fuel type capability
notes: Duplicate entry of most proven technology, currently operating at required scale at La Hague
gap_analysis: Perfect alignment - most mature industrial method for uranium/plutonium extraction with proven capability for all required fuel types.

name: COEX (Co-Extraction) Process
description: Modified PUREX process that co-extracts uranium and plutonium together rather than separately, designed to reduce proliferation risks while maintaining reprocessing efficiency for fuel cycle applications.
function_alignment: High - addresses same function with enhanced proliferation resistance, suitable for MOX and multiple fuel types
notes: Duplicate entry - CEA-developed evolution of PUREX with enhanced safeguards but less commercial deployment
gap_analysis: Strong alignment with enhanced proliferation resistance. Achieves same function as PUREX with improved safeguards characteristics.
